name: Shekinah
shortDescription: The Shekinah represents the divine feminine presence, often associated with intuition, hidden knowledge, and the unconscious. In Tarot, it symbolizes the receptive and nurturing aspect of the Divine.
description: The Shekinah, derived from Hebrew, signifies the dwelling or settling of the divine presence of God. It is often understood as the feminine aspect of God, immanent within the world. In the context of Tarot, particularly within esoteric and occult interpretations, the Shekinah is closely linked to the High Priestess card. This card represents intuition, hidden knowledge, and the exploration of the unconscious realm. The Shekinah embodies the receptive and nurturing qualities of the divine, offering insight and guidance to those who seek deeper understanding. Its connection to the High Priestess emphasizes the importance of inner wisdom and the ability to access spiritual truths beyond the realm of conscious thought. The Shekinah is also associated with mystery, feminine spirituality, and the veil between the conscious and unconscious. Within the Tarot, understanding the Shekinah allows one to delve into the hidden aspects of the self and the universe, facilitating a deeper connection with the divine feminine and accessing profound spiritual knowledge. It encourages introspection, reflection, and the exploration of the unseen forces that shape our reality. It acts as a bridge between the earthly and the divine, offering solace, guidance, and profound spiritual insight. The Shekinah's presence in Tarot emphasizes the power of receptivity, intuition, and the importance of honoring the feminine aspect of the divine within ourselves and the world around us.

sources.title: Kabbalah: Key to Your Inner Power
sources.author: Elizabeth Clare Prophet
